todaysEvents.summary: US submarine sank Iranian frigate Iris Dena off Sri Lanka — 87 bodies recovered, 32 rescued. Naval combat has now expanded far beyond the Persian Gulf into the Indian Ocean, marking a qualitative escalation in the war's geographic scope. Kurdish Iranian factions backed by CIA and Mossad are preparing for a possible ground offensive in northwest Iran per Axios and CNN — no offensive yet confirmed launched (Mar 6)
todaysEvents.delta: US sub sinks Iranian frigate / Indian Ocean war

todaysEvents.summary: Iraq cut oil output by ~1.5M bbl/day due to storage and export constraints with Hormuz blocked; Reuters reports Kuwait and UAE may next reduce output — combined GCC cuts could exceed 3M+ bpd within days, adding unintended supply shock on top of Hormuz disruption. Brent surged +3% to $85.60/bbl; WTI +6% to $81.20/bbl. House and Senate both rejected war powers resolution — US military authority to continue Iran campaign confirmed. Hegseth: US is 'accelerating, not decelerating' war (Mar 6)

Qatar FM made first direct diplomatic contact with Iranian FM since fighting began — only de-escalation signal of Day 7. Trump said he 'should be involved' in selecting Iran's next Supreme Leader, complicating negotiations. 329 tankers remain stuck in the Gulf. Iran death toll at 1,045 with 6,000+ wounded per Iranian state media. US Embassy in Kuwait closed by State Dept following Iranian strikes in the region (military.com Mar 5). P&I blackout remains total — no commercial insurance market for Gulf transits exists (Mar 6)
todaysEvents.delta: Qatar FM opens channel / Iran 1,045 dead

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Hormuz disruption lasts 2–4 weeks; Qatar FM back-channel leads to ceasefire framework within 10–14 days. Trump's naval escort program partially restores tanker flow. Brent stabilises at $82–92/bbl with sustained risk premium. Saudi/UAE spare capacity (3-week ramp) partially offsets supply deficit after Hormuz reopens. ASEAN LNG buyers source spot cargoes from Australian and US suppliers. Iraq gradually restores output once tanker transit resumes. P&I clubs restore cover upon ceasefire confirmation. Goldman Q4 2026 normalisation forecast at $60/bbl contingent on swift resolution.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Hormuz closure extends 1–3 months; Indian Ocean front expands with further IRGC naval actions. P&I blackout proves durable; 329 trapped tankers cleared only partially. Iraq output loss widens to 3M+ bpd; Kuwait/UAE also cut. Brent surges toward $100–115/bbl. JKM LNG spikes to $25+/MMBtu — worst gas market shock since Russia-Ukraine 2022. ASEAN current account deficits widen sharply; Bank Indonesia and BSP forced into emergency interventions. Indonesian subsidy blowout accelerates. Kurdish front in northwest Iran prolongs conflict.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Full Hormuz closure sustained beyond 3 months; IRGC acts on Malacca threat; Indian Ocean naval escalation draws in additional parties. Oil at $150–200/bbl (J.P. Morgan upper bound). LNG effectively unavailable for spot buyers; Qatar's 77 mtpa offline. Iraq, Kuwait, UAE combined output loss exceeds 6M bpd — global supply deficit unprecedented since 1973. Recession probability >70% for Thailand and Philippines. ASEAN EM bonds face mass outflows; P&I cover does not return for 6+ months; global trade finance seizes.
